title: stachys nivea subsp. masandarana
intro: Stachys nivea subsp. masandarana, also known as Masandaran White Woundwort, is a plant species within the mint family (Lamiaceae). It is a perennial herb native to the mountainous regions of Iran, known for its medicinal properties, particularly its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory qualities. The plant has been used in traditional medicine to treat various health issues, including wounds, skin infections, and respiratory problems, due to its ability to promote tissue repair and reduce inflammation. Stachys nivea subsp. masandarana can be prepared in various herbal forms, such as tea, tincture, or salve, which can be used topically or ingested to harness its medicinal benefits.
